name: litejsondb

metadata.name: LiteJsonDb
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Application Frameworks
metadata.summary: A lightweight JSON-based database system. It's designed for simplicity and ease of use.

documentation: Docs available here: https://github.com/codingtuto/LiteJsonDb/

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.modules.tgbot.BackupToTelegram.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the BackupToTelegram class.

Parameters:
- token (str): The access token for your Telegram bot.
- chat_id (str): The Telegram chat ID where the file will be sent.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.modules.tgbot.BackupToTelegram._send_request
documentation:
Internal helper function to send the backup file to the Telegram chat.

Parameters:
- files (dict): The backup file to be sent.
- caption (str): A message to accompany the file, in HTML format.

Returns:
- bool: True if the file was successfully sent, False otherwise.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.modules.tgbot.BackupToTelegram.backup_to_telegram
documentation:
Back up a file by sending it to a Telegram chat.

Parameters:
- backup_filepath (str): The path to the backup file you want to send.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.modules.csv.CSVExporter.export
documentation: EXPORT DATA TO CSV
Exports JSON data to a CSV file. Supports either
a single collection or an entire database.

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.convert_to_datetime
documentation: Turn that boring string into a fancy datetime object! Time travel not included.

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.get_or_default
documentation: Can't find what you're looking for? Here's your fallback! Retrieves a value or serves a default one if the key is missing in action.

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.key_exists_or_add
documentation: Check if a key exists, and if not, sneakily add it with a default value. It's like a secret ingredient!

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.normalize_keys
documentation: Normalize those wild keys (like all lowercase). Because case sensitivity is sooo last season.

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.flatten_json
documentation: Squish that nested JSON into a flat dictionary. Like a JSON pancake! 🥞

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.filter_data
documentation: Filter out the riffraff based on a condition. Only the finest data shall pass!

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.sort_data
documentation: Sort that data like a pro. Whether ascending or descending, we've got your back!

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.hash_password
documentation: Turn your plain password into a cryptographic masterpiece using SHA-256. Hackers, beware!

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.check_password
documentation: Verify if the password matches the stored hash. The ultimate password bouncer!

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.sanitize_output
documentation:
Sanitize the output to avoid XSS vulnerabilities. Keep your app safe from those sneaky hackers! 
You're probably thinking, 'XSS vulnerability in Python, huh?' Yeah, I discovered this on a LinkedIn post and thought, why not include it? Maybe...there are people who might use our tool as a database for their web apps🙃

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.utils.pretty_print
documentation: Make your data shine with beautifully formatted output. It's like a makeover for your JSON!

functions.name: LiteJsonDb.modules.search.search_data
documentation:
Search for a value in a nested dictionary or within a specific key.

```
param data

The dictionary to search within.

param search_value

The value to search for.

param key

Optional. If provided, search within this specific key.

return

A dictionary containing matching results.
```

documentation: Decode data from base64.

documentation: Encrypt data using Fernet.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB._decrypt_fernet
documentation: Decrypt data using Fernet.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB._load_db
documentation: Load the database from the JSON file, or create a new one if it doesn't exist.
DATABASE OPERATIONS
SAVE, RESTORE, AND RETRIEVE

documentation: Save the database to the JSON file.

documentation: Create a backup of the database.

documentation: Create db backup using Telegram API

Exports either a specified collection or the entire
database to a CSV file.

DATA VALIDATION

documentation: Helper to set data in a nested dictionary.

documentation: Merge dict2 into dict1.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.key_exists
documentation: Check if a key exists in the database.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.get_data
documentation: Get data from the database by key.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.set_data
documentation:
Set data in the database and notify observers.

If `value` is not provided, initialize the key with an empty dictionary.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.edit_data
documentation: Edit data in the database and notify observers.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.add_observer
documentation: Add an observer for a specific key.
DATA OBSERVERS

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.remove_observer
documentation: Remove an observer for a specific key.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.notify_observers
documentation: Notify all observers about a change.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.remove_data
documentation: Remove data from the database by key.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.get_db
documentation: Get the entire database, optionally in raw format.
WHOLE DATABASE

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.get_subcollection
documentation: Get a specific subcollection or an item within a subcollection.
SUBCOLLECTION VALIDATION

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.set_subcollection
documentation: Set an item in a specific subcollection.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.edit_subcollection
documentation: Edit an item in a specific subcollection.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.remove_subcollection
documentation: Remove an entire subcollection or a specific item within it.

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.search_data
documentation:
Search for a value in the database.

```
param value

The elusive treasure you're hunting for.

param key

A specific key to search within the documents. If None, we'll search everywhere like a treasure map with no boundaries.

return

A dictionary of found items or None if the treasure remains hidden.
```
DATA SEARCH

methods.name: LiteJsonDb.LiteJsonDb.JsonDB.call_utility_function
documentation: UTILITY FUNCTIONS
(Methods for commonly used utilities)
This section provides static methods that act as
wrappers for various utility functions. They are
designed to be used across different parts of the
application for convenience and consistency.
